ReneWind - Wind Turbine Failure Prediction¶

Business Context¶

Renewable energy and predictive maintenance are central to reducing downtime and operational costs. ReneWind wants to identify generator failures early so components can be repaired before replacement becomes necessary.

Objective¶

Build neural-network classification models to predict generator failure (1 = failure, 0 = no failure), improve performance through multiple modeling strategies, and select the best model using both ML metrics and a cost-aware business criterion.

Rubric Coverage Map¶

  • EDA: Data overview, univariate, bivariate
  • Preprocessing: Missing-value treatment and leakage control
  • Baseline NN: SGD optimizer + rationale
  • Performance improvement: 6+ experiments using depth/optimizer/dropout/class weights
  • Final model: Test-set evaluation and business recommendations

1. Environment Setup and Reproducibility¶

We import required libraries, set random seeds, and define helper utilities for consistent experiment tracking and cost-aware evaluation.

In [1]:
import os
import random
import warnings
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.metrics import (
    accuracy_score,
    precision_score,
    recall_score,
    f1_score,
    roc_auc_score,
    average_precision_score,
    confusion_matrix,
    classification_report,
    RocCurveDisplay,
    PrecisionRecallDisplay,
)
from sklearn.utils.class_weight import compute_class_weight

import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense, Dropout, Input
from tensorflow.keras.optimizers import SGD, Adam
from tensorflow.keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

SEED = 42
random.seed(SEED)
np.random.seed(SEED)
tf.random.set_seed(SEED)

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (10, 5)

print("TensorFlow version:", tf.__version__)
print("Seed set to:", SEED)

TensorFlow version: 2.21.0
Seed set to: 42
In [2]:
# Business cost assumptions (can be updated based on real contracts)
COST_FP = 5    # Inspection cost for false alarm
COST_TP = 20   # Repair cost for correctly predicted failure
COST_FN = 100  # Replacement cost for missed failure


def to_dense(x):
    return x.toarray() if hasattr(x, "toarray") else x


def compute_business_cost(y_true, y_pred):
    tn, fp, fn, tp = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred).ravel()
    total_cost = (COST_FN * fn) + (COST_TP * tp) + (COST_FP * fp)
    return {
        "TN": tn,
        "FP": fp,
        "FN": fn,
        "TP": tp,
        "Business_Cost": total_cost,
    }


def evaluate_predictions(y_true, y_prob, threshold=0.5):
    y_pred = (y_prob >= threshold).astype(int)
    stats = {
        "Accuracy": accuracy_score(y_true, y_pred),
        "Precision": precision_score(y_true, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "Recall": recall_score(y_true, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "F1": f1_score(y_true, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "ROC_AUC": roc_auc_score(y_true, y_prob),
        "PR_AUC": average_precision_score(y_true, y_prob),
        "Threshold": threshold,
    }
    stats.update(compute_business_cost(y_true, y_pred))
    return stats


def build_model(input_dim, hidden_layers=(64,), optimizer_name="sgd", dropout_rate=0.0, lr=0.01):
    tf.keras.backend.clear_session()

    model = Sequential()
    model.add(Input(shape=(input_dim,)))

    for units in hidden_layers:
        model.add(Dense(units, activation="relu"))
        if dropout_rate > 0:
            model.add(Dropout(dropout_rate))

    model.add(Dense(1, activation="sigmoid"))

    if optimizer_name.lower() == "adam":
        optimizer = Adam(learning_rate=lr)
    else:
        optimizer = SGD(learning_rate=lr, momentum=0.9)

    model.compile(
        optimizer=optimizer,
        loss="binary_crossentropy",
        metrics=[tf.keras.metrics.AUC(name="auc")],
    )
    return model

2. Load Train/Test Data from Provided File Paths¶

We load the transformed confidential sensor datasets and verify schema expectations.

In [3]:
train_path = "/Users/lakshmanraghu/Documents/Great Learning/Project - Neural Networks/datasets/Train.csv"
test_path = "/Users/lakshmanraghu/Documents/Great Learning/Project - Neural Networks/datasets/Test.csv"

5. Bivariate Analysis Against Failure Target¶

We compare feature behavior across failure classes and inspect correlation patterns.

In [6]:
top_var_cols = train_df[numeric_cols].var().sort_values(ascending=False).head(6).index.tolist() if numeric_cols else []

if top_var_cols:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(16, 8))
    axes = axes.flatten()
    for i, col in enumerate(top_var_cols):
        sns.boxplot(data=train_df, x=target_col, y=col, ax=axes[i], palette="Set3")
        axes[i].set_title(f"{col} vs Target")
    for j in range(i + 1, len(axes)):
        axes[j].axis("off")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(16, 8))
    axes = axes.flatten()
    for i, col in enumerate(top_var_cols):
        sns.violinplot(data=train_df, x=target_col, y=col, ax=axes[i], palette="Set2", cut=0)
        axes[i].set_title(f"{col} distribution by class")
    for j in range(i + 1, len(axes)):
        axes[j].axis("off")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

corr_subset = numeric_cols[:15] if len(numeric_cols) > 15 else numeric_cols
if corr_subset:
    corr_df = train_df[corr_subset + [target_col]].corr()
    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))
    sns.heatmap(corr_df, cmap="coolwarm", center=0)
    plt.title("Correlation Heatmap (subset + target)")
    plt.show()

if corr_subset:
    target_corr = corr_df[target_col].drop(target_col).sort_values(key=np.abs, ascending=False)
    print("Top absolute correlations with target:")
    display(target_corr.head(10))

6. Preprocessing Pipeline (Missing Values, Scaling, Leakage Control)¶

We split training data into train/validation using stratification, fit preprocessing on train split only, and transform validation/test without leakage.

In [7]:
X = train_df.drop(columns=[target_col]).copy()
y = train_df[target_col].astype(int).copy()

X_test_final = test_df.drop(columns=[target_col]).copy()
y_test_final = test_df[target_col].astype(int).copy()

X_train_raw, X_val_raw, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(
    X,
    y,
    test_size=0.2,
    random_state=SEED,
    stratify=y,
)

numeric_features = X_train_raw.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns.tolist()
categorical_features = [c for c in X_train_raw.columns if c not in numeric_features]

numeric_transformer = Pipeline(
    steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
        ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ]
)

categorical_transformer = Pipeline(
    steps=[
        ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
        ("onehot", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore")),
    ]
)

preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ("num", numeric_transformer, numeric_features),
        ("cat", categorical_transformer, categorical_features),
    ]
)

X_train_proc = to_dense(preprocessor.fit_transform(X_train_raw))
X_val_proc = to_dense(preprocessor.transform(X_val_raw))
X_test_proc = to_dense(preprocessor.transform(X_test_final))

print("Train split shape:", X_train_raw.shape)
print("Validation split shape:", X_val_raw.shape)
print("Processed train shape:", X_train_proc.shape)
print("Processed validation shape:", X_val_proc.shape)
print("Processed test shape:", X_test_proc.shape)

Train split shape: (16000, 40)
Validation split shape: (4000, 40)
Processed train shape: (16000, 40)
Processed validation shape: (4000, 40)
Processed test shape: (5000, 40)

7. Metric Design with Cost-Sensitive Rationale¶

Accuracy alone is insufficient for predictive maintenance. We prioritize:

  • Recall (class 1) to reduce missed failures (false negatives)
  • F1 for precision-recall balance
  • PR-AUC for minority/failure detection quality
  • Business Cost where $C_{FN} > C_{TP} > C_{FP}$

We use: $$ \text{Cost} = C_{FN}\cdot FN + C_{TP}\cdot TP + C_{FP}\cdot FP $$ with $C_{FN}=100$, $C_{TP}=20$, $C_{FP}=5$.

In [8]:
def run_experiment(name, hidden_layers, optimizer_name, dropout_rate, class_weight=None, lr=0.01, epochs=30):
    model = build_model(
        input_dim=X_train_proc.shape[1],
        hidden_layers=hidden_layers,
        optimizer_name=optimizer_name,
        dropout_rate=dropout_rate,
        lr=lr,
    )

    early_stop = EarlyStopping(
        monitor="val_loss",
        mode="min",
        patience=4,
        restore_best_weights=True,
        verbose=0,
    )

    history = model.fit(
        X_train_proc,
        y_train,
        validation_data=(X_val_proc, y_val),
        epochs=epochs,
        batch_size=256,
        verbose=0,
        callbacks=[early_stop],
        class_weight=class_weight,
    )

    y_val_prob = model.predict(X_val_proc, verbose=0).ravel()
    stats = evaluate_predictions(y_val, y_val_prob, threshold=0.5)
    stats["Model"] = name
    stats["Layers"] = str(hidden_layers)
    stats["Optimizer"] = optimizer_name
    stats["Dropout"] = dropout_rate
    stats["Class_Weight"] = "Yes" if class_weight else "No"
    stats["Best_Epoch"] = int(np.argmin(history.history["val_loss"]) + 1)

    return model, history, stats, y_val_prob


class_weights_vals = compute_class_weight(
    class_weight="balanced",
    classes=np.unique(y_train),
    y=y_train,
)
class_weight_dict = {int(k): float(v) for k, v in zip(np.unique(y_train), class_weights_vals)}

print("Class weights:", class_weight_dict)
print(f"Cost formula uses FN={COST_FN}, TP={COST_TP}, FP={COST_FP}")

Class weights: {0: 0.5293806246691372, 1: 9.00900900900901}
Cost formula uses FN=100, TP=20, FP=5

8. Baseline Neural Network (SGD Optimizer)¶

Baseline architecture: one hidden layer using SGD optimizer, with early stopping.

In [9]:
models = {}
histories = {}
val_probs = {}
results = []

baseline_model, baseline_hist, baseline_stats, baseline_prob = run_experiment(
    name="Baseline_SGD",
    hidden_layers=(64,),
    optimizer_name="sgd",
    dropout_rate=0.0,
    class_weight=None,
    lr=0.01,
)

models["Baseline_SGD"] = baseline_model
histories["Baseline_SGD"] = baseline_hist
val_probs["Baseline_SGD"] = baseline_prob
results.append(baseline_stats)

display(pd.DataFrame([baseline_stats]).set_index("Model"))

# Threshold behavior snapshot
for th in [0.30, 0.50, 0.70]:
    th_stats = evaluate_predictions(y_val, baseline_prob, threshold=th)
    print(
        f"Threshold={th:.2f} | Recall={th_stats['Recall']:.3f} | "
        f"Precision={th_stats['Precision']:.3f} | FN={th_stats['FN']} | Cost={th_stats['Business_Cost']}"
    )
Accuracy Precision Recall F1 ROC_AUC PR_AUC Threshold TN FP FN TP Business_Cost Layers Optimizer Dropout Class_Weight Best_Epoch
Model
Baseline_SGD 0.9895 0.98913 0.81982 0.896552 0.939969 0.913524 0.5 3776 2 40 182 7650 (64,) sgd 0.0 No 30 
Threshold=0.30 | Recall=0.883 | Precision=0.951 | FN=26 | Cost=6570

Threshold=0.70 | Recall=0.730 | Precision=0.994 | FN=60 | Cost=9245

9-14. Model Performance Improvement Experiments¶

We run six required improvement experiments using combinations of:

  • more hidden layers
  • optimizer change (SGD/Adam)
  • dropout
  • class weights
In [10]:
experiment_configs = [
    {
        "name": "Exp1_MoreLayers_SGD",
        "hidden_layers": (128, 64),
        "optimizer_name": "sgd",
        "dropout_rate": 0.0,
        "class_weight": None,
        "lr": 0.01,
        "note": "More hidden layers with SGD",
    },
    {
        "name": "Exp2_Adam",
        "hidden_layers": (64,),
        "optimizer_name": "adam",
        "dropout_rate": 0.0,
        "class_weight": None,
        "lr": 0.001,
        "note": "Optimizer changed from SGD to Adam",
    },
    {
        "name": "Exp3_Dropout",
        "hidden_layers": (64,),
        "optimizer_name": "adam",
        "dropout_rate": 0.3,
        "class_weight": None,
        "lr": 0.001,
        "note": "Dropout regularization",
    },
    {
        "name": "Exp4_ClassWeights",
        "hidden_layers": (64,),
        "optimizer_name": "adam",
        "dropout_rate": 0.0,
        "class_weight": class_weight_dict,
        "lr": 0.001,
        "note": "Class weights for imbalance",
    },
    {
        "name": "Exp5_MoreLayers_Adam_Dropout",
        "hidden_layers": (128, 64),
        "optimizer_name": "adam",
        "dropout_rate": 0.3,
        "class_weight": None,
        "lr": 0.001,
        "note": "Deeper model + Adam + dropout",
    },
    {
        "name": "Exp6_AllCombined",
        "hidden_layers": (128, 64, 32),
        "optimizer_name": "adam",
        "dropout_rate": 0.3,
        "class_weight": class_weight_dict,
        "lr": 0.001,
        "note": "Depth + Adam + dropout + class weights",
    },
]

for cfg in experiment_configs:
    model, hist, stats, y_prob = run_experiment(
        name=cfg["name"],
        hidden_layers=cfg["hidden_layers"],
        optimizer_name=cfg["optimizer_name"],
        dropout_rate=cfg["dropout_rate"],
        class_weight=cfg["class_weight"],
        lr=cfg["lr"],
    )
    stats["Note"] = cfg["note"]

    models[cfg["name"]] = model
    histories[cfg["name"]] = hist
    val_probs[cfg["name"]] = y_prob
    results.append(stats)

    print(f"Completed: {cfg['name']}")
    print(
        f"Recall={stats['Recall']:.3f}, F1={stats['F1']:.3f}, "
        f"PR_AUC={stats['PR_AUC']:.3f}, FN={stats['FN']}, Cost={stats['Business_Cost']}"
    )
    print("-" * 80)

15. Experiment Tracking, Comparison, and Best Model Selection¶

Primary selection criterion: minimum business cost, with high recall for failure class and strong PR-AUC as tie-breakers.

In [11]:
results_df = pd.DataFrame(results)
cols_order = [
    "Model", "Layers", "Optimizer", "Dropout", "Class_Weight", "Best_Epoch",
    "Accuracy", "Precision", "Recall", "F1", "ROC_AUC", "PR_AUC",
    "FN", "FP", "TP", "TN", "Business_Cost", "Note"
]
results_df = results_df[cols_order].sort_values(by=["Business_Cost", "FN", "PR_AUC"], ascending=[True, True, False])

display(results_df.style.background_gradient(subset=["Recall", "F1", "PR_AUC"], cmap="Greens"))

best_model_name = results_df.iloc[0]["Model"]
print("Selected best model:", best_model_name)
print("Selection rationale: lowest business cost with strong failure recall and PR-AUC.")

In [12]:
# Visual comparison for key metrics
plot_df = results_df.copy()
metrics_plot = ["Recall", "Precision", "F1", "PR_AUC", "Business_Cost"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(metrics_plot), figsize=(24, 4))
for ax, metric in zip(axes, metrics_plot):
    sorted_df = plot_df.sort_values(metric, ascending=(metric == "Business_Cost"))
    sns.barplot(data=sorted_df, x="Model", y=metric, ax=ax, palette="viridis")
    ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=80)
    ax.set_title(metric)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

16. Final Test-Set Evaluation and Error-Cost Analysis¶

We retrain the selected architecture using full training data (Train.csv), then evaluate exactly once on Test.csv.

In [13]:
best_row = results_df.loc[results_df["Model"] == best_model_name].iloc[0]

# Refit preprocessor on full training data (no test leakage)
X_full = train_df.drop(columns=[target_col]).copy()
y_full = train_df[target_col].astype(int).copy()

X_test_holdout = test_df.drop(columns=[target_col]).copy()
y_test_holdout = test_df[target_col].astype(int).copy()

num_full = X_full.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns.tolist()
cat_full = [c for c in X_full.columns if c not in num_full]

full_preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ("num", Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
        ]), num_full),
        ("cat", Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
            ("onehot", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore")),
        ]), cat_full),
    ]
)

X_full_proc = to_dense(full_preprocessor.fit_transform(X_full))
X_test_holdout_proc = to_dense(full_preprocessor.transform(X_test_holdout))

final_model = build_model(
    input_dim=X_full_proc.shape[1],
    hidden_layers=eval(best_row["Layers"]),
    optimizer_name=best_row["Optimizer"],
    dropout_rate=float(best_row["Dropout"]),
    lr=0.001 if str(best_row["Optimizer"]).lower() == "adam" else 0.01,
)

final_class_weight = class_weight_dict if best_row["Class_Weight"] == "Yes" else None

final_es = EarlyStopping(monitor="val_loss", mode="min", patience=4, restore_best_weights=True, verbose=0)

X_subtrain, X_subval, y_subtrain, y_subval = train_test_split(
    X_full_proc, y_full, test_size=0.15, random_state=SEED, stratify=y_full
)

final_history = final_model.fit(
    X_subtrain,
    y_subtrain,
    validation_data=(X_subval, y_subval),
    epochs=35,
    batch_size=256,
    verbose=0,
    callbacks=[final_es],
    class_weight=final_class_weight,
)

y_test_prob = final_model.predict(X_test_holdout_proc, verbose=0).ravel()
final_test_stats = evaluate_predictions(y_test_holdout, y_test_prob, threshold=0.5)

print("Final Test Metrics:")
for k in ["Accuracy", "Precision", "Recall", "F1", "ROC_AUC", "PR_AUC", "FN", "FP", "TP", "TN", "Business_Cost"]:
    v = final_test_stats[k]
    if isinstance(v, float):
        print(f"{k}: {v:.4f}")
    else:
        print(f"{k}: {v}")

print("\nClassification Report (Test):")
print(classification_report(y_test_holdout, (y_test_prob >= 0.5).astype(int), digits=4))

In [14]:
y_test_pred = (y_test_prob >= 0.5).astype(int)
cm = confusion_matrix(y_test_holdout, y_test_pred)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt="d", cmap="Blues", ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title("Confusion Matrix (Test)")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Predicted")
axes[0].set_ylabel("Actual")

cm_norm = confusion_matrix(y_test_holdout, y_test_pred, normalize="true")
sns.heatmap(cm_norm, annot=True, fmt=".2%", cmap="Greens", ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title("Normalized Confusion Matrix (Test)")
axes[1].set_xlabel("Predicted")
axes[1].set_ylabel("Actual")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
RocCurveDisplay.from_predictions(y_test_holdout, y_test_prob, ax=axes[0])
axes[0].plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", alpha=0.6)
axes[0].set_title("ROC Curve (Test)")

PrecisionRecallDisplay.from_predictions(y_test_holdout, y_test_prob, ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title("Precision-Recall Curve (Test)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("Cost interpretation:")
print(f"Total modeled operational cost on Test set = {final_test_stats['Business_Cost']}")
print("Lower is better. FN is heavily penalized to reflect expensive unplanned replacement.")

Cost interpretation:
Total modeled operational cost on Test set = 8560
Lower is better. FN is heavily penalized to reflect expensive unplanned replacement.

17. Actionable Recommendations from Model Outputs¶

Maintenance Actions¶

  1. Use model predictions to trigger proactive repair workflow for high-risk turbines (predicted failure = 1).
  2. Prioritize inspection queues by predicted probability; inspect highest-risk units first.
  3. Treat false negatives as the most critical error because missed failures drive replacement cost.

Threshold and Cost Policy¶

  1. If business prefers fewer missed failures, lower threshold below 0.50 and accept more inspections.
  2. Recalibrate threshold monthly based on latest repair/replacement/inspection cost ratios.
  3. Monitor FN trend as a leading KPI for reliability risk.

Model Governance¶

  1. Track data drift in sensor distributions and class balance.
  2. Retrain periodically (e.g., monthly/quarterly) or when performance drops.
  3. Maintain a champion-challenger setup using the best current model versus new experimental variants.

Conclusion¶

A cost-sensitive neural-network framework was built with leakage-safe preprocessing, multiple architecture/optimizer/regularization experiments, and test-set validation. The selected model provides an actionable basis for reducing high-cost failures through earlier intervention.
